message.content:
You are an ADVERSARIAL verifier for HC-GRC hardening PASS 6. Repo root: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc, branch hardening/pass-6. Your job is to REFUTE the following candidate finding by reading the real code. Default to confirmed=false unless you can mechanically prove the defect is real, reachable in practice, and NOT already mitigated elsewhere.

CANDIDATE (dimension xc-tests):
title: Missing concurrent access test for checkpointer _init_lock
severity: medium
location: tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py:91-110
problem: The test test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str verifies sequential idempotency (two calls in sequence), but the code explicitly documents that _init_lock protects against concurrent threads calling get_checkpointer(). No test exists for actual concurrent/threaded access, leaving the critical race condition protection untested.
best_practice_violated: Cross-layer synchronization primitives (locks) must have explicit test coverage for the concurrent scenarios they protect. Documentation mentions concurrent initialization but tests only cover sequential calls.
proposed_fix: Add a test that spawns multiple threads, each calling get_checkpointer(), and verifies that setup() is called exactly once even with concurrent access. Use threading.Thread and a barrier to ensure simultaneous invocation.

Verify against the actual code (Read/Grep/Bash read-only, inspect installed deps if needed). Check: is the cited code really as described? Is the failure path reachable? Is it already guarded/mitigated by other code (timeouts, validation, reducers, idempotency guards)? Is the severity right? Return the verdict; set confirmed=false for false positives or already-fixed items and say so explicitly.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me read the test file directly:

content.content:
1	"""
2	Checkpointer factory tests (hardening pass 1, #161).
3	
4	MemorySaver is always exercised. The PostgresSaver path (self-healing pool) is
5	exercised and round-tripped against real Postgres. In CI a Postgres service
6	container is provisioned (see .github/workflows/test.yml), so these run there too;
7	locally they skip only when no database is reachable (#260).
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import os
13	
14	import pytest
15	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
16	
17	from src.checkpointer import get_checkpointer, get_postgres_connection_string
18	from src.state import initial_state
19	
20	# GitHub Actions sets GITHUB_ACTIONS=true. In CI the durability/recovery invariant
21	# MUST be exercised, not silently skipped — a plain skipif on a DB-less runner left
22	# the cross-boundary recovery contract unverified exactly where regressions land
23	# (#260). Locally (no CI), missing Postgres still skips so contributors without a
24	# database can run the rest of the suite.
25	_IN_CI = os.environ.get("GITHUB_ACTIONS", "").lower() == "true"
26	
27	
28	def test_memory_checkpointer():
29	    cp = get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)
30	    assert isinstance(cp, MemorySaver)
31	
32	
33	def _postgres_reachable() -> bool:
34	    try:
35	        import psycopg
36	        with psycopg.connect(get_postgres_connection_string(), connect_timeout=2):
37	            return True
38	    except Exception:
39	        return False
40	
41	
42	def _require_postgres() -> None:
43	    """Skip locally when Postgres is absent, but FAIL in CI — the durability
44	    invariant must never silently skip on the runner where regressions slip in.
45	
46	    Evaluated at call time (in the test body), not at import time like a skipif
47	    decorator, so a service container that finishes coming up just after collection
48	    is still picked up (#260)."""
49	    if _postgres_reachable():
50	        return
51	    if _IN_CI:
52	        pytest.fail(
53	            "Postgres is unreachable in CI — the checkpointer durability/recovery "
54	            "tests must run here (provision the service container and set "
55	            "HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL). The cross-boundary recovery invariant must not "
56	            "silently skip in CI (#260)."
57	        )
58	    pytest.skip("Postgres not reachable (local run without a database)")
59	
60	
61	def test_postgres_checkpointer_round_trips():
62	    _require_postgres()
63	    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
64	
65	    cp = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
66	    assert isinstance(cp, PostgresSaver)
67	    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "test-cp-roundtrip", "checkpoint_ns": ""}}
68	    # list() must succeed against the pooled connection (no error == healthy pool).
69	    assert isinstance(list(cp.list(cfg)), list)
70	
71	
72	def test_checkpoint_recovers_across_a_fresh_checkpointer():
73	    # Durability across a process/connection boundary (#224): write a checkpoint
74	    # with one checkpointer/pool, then read it back through a SEPARATE checkpointer
75	    # (new pool) — simulating a process restart resuming a parked run.
76	    _require_postgres()
77	    from src.graph import build_graph
78	
79	    run_id = "test-cp-recovery"
80	    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": run_id}}
81	
82	    cp_write = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
83	    build_graph(checkpointer=cp_write).invoke(initial_state(run_id=run_id), config=cfg)
84	
85	    cp_read = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)  # fresh pool / different connections
86	    recovered = build_graph(checkpointer=cp_read).get_state(cfg)
87	    assert recovered.values.get("run_id") == run_id
88	    assert recovered.next  # still parked at the Gate 1 interrupt
89	
90	
91	def test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str(monkeypatch):
92	    # #276 (pass-5): setup() must run once per process per conn_str, not on every
93	    # get_checkpointer() call. Counts real setup() invocations across two calls.
94	    _require_postgres()
95	    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
96	
97	    import src.checkpointer as cp_mod
98	
99	    cp_mod._INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.clear()
100	    calls = {"n": 0}
101	    orig = PostgresSaver.setup
102	
103	    def counting(self):
104	        calls["n"] += 1
105	        return orig(self)
106	
107	    monkeypatch.setattr(PostgresSaver, "setup", counting)
108	    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
109	    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
110	    assert calls["n"] == 1  # second call hits the _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS guard
111	
112	
113	def test_get_checkpointer_raises_helpful_error_on_connection_failure(monkeypatch):
114	    # The error path (lines wrapping ConnectionPool/setup) must surface the
115	    # operator-facing RuntimeError with the "is Postgres running" guidance. Mocks
116	    # ConnectionPool so it needs no real database.
117	    import psycopg_pool
118	
119	    def boom(*a, **k):
120	        raise RuntimeError("connection refused")
121	
122	    monkeypatch.setattr(psycopg_pool, "ConnectionPool", boom)
123	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="PostgresSaver connection failed"):
124	        get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
125

content.text: Now let me check the actual implementation of `get_checkpointer()` to understand how the `_init_lock` is used:

content.content:
1	"""
2	PostgresSaver configuration for HC-GRC.
3	
4	LangGraph uses the checkpointer to persist graph state between turns,
5	enabling interrupt/resume across the operator approval gates.
6	
7	Per ADR-0015 (#79): run_id propagates to PostgresSaver as thread_id.
8	Cross-store query procedure in RUNBOOK.md §5.
9	
10	LOCAL-FIRST CONSTRAINT: PostgreSQL instance runs on-device.
11	No state leaves the machine. SaaS checkpointers are disqualified.
12	
13	Connection config:
14	  Database: hcgrc
15	  Host: localhost (default)
16	  Port: 5432 (default)
17	
18	For Phase 0 testing: use MemorySaver (no Postgres required).
19	For Phase 1+: use PostgresSaver with the connection string below.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import atexit
25	import os
26	import threading
27	
28	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
29	
30	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes
31	# checkpoint schema migrations across concurrent initializers (get_checkpointer).
32	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4843_4752  # "HCGR"
33	
34	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping below. The PG advisory lock
35	# serializes the DDL across PROCESSES, but the in-memory check-and-set guards
36	# (_atexit_registered, _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS) are not atomic on their own — two
37	# threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and
38	# double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup(). This lock makes those
39	# check-and-set sequences atomic within the process (pass-5).
40	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
41	
42	# Pools opened by get_checkpointer, closed once at process exit. A single atexit
43	# handler closes them all — registering one per call would accumulate handlers
44	# across repeated get_checkpointer() calls (#249).
45	_OPEN_POOLS: list = []
46	_atexit_registered = False
47	
48	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL + migration SELECT)
49	# has already run in this process. setup() is idempotent but not free — re-running
50	# it on every get_checkpointer() call takes a global advisory lock and replays the
51	# migration version query at each run start. Bootstrap once per process per schema;
52	# the advisory lock still guards the first-time cross-process migration race (#276).
53	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
54	
55	
56	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool) -> None:
57	    global _atexit_registered
58	    with _init_lock:
59	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
60	        if not _atexit_registered:
61	            atexit.register(lambda: [p.close() for p in _OPEN_POOLS])
62	            _atexit_registered = True
63	
64	# ── Connection string ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	
66	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
67	    """
68	    Return the PostgreSQL connection string.
69	
70	    Precedence: HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var → the checkpointing.{host,port,database}
71	    section of platform.yaml → localhost default. This makes the platform.yaml
72	    checkpointing config live instead of dead (#225). Never logged, never committed.
73	    """
74	    env_url = os.environ.get("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL")
75	    if env_url:
76	        return env_url
77	    try:
78	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
79	        cp = load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}
80	        host = cp.get("host", "localhost")
81	        port = cp.get("port", 5432)
82	        database = cp.get("database", "hcgrc")
83	        return f"postgresql://{host}:{port}/{database}"
84	    except Exception:
85	        return "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc"
86	
87	
88	def configured_checkpointer():
89	    """
90	    Return the checkpointer the platform config asks for: MemorySaver when
91	    checkpointing.backend == 'memory', else the pooled PostgresSaver (#226).
92	    Runners use this as the default instead of hardcoding in-memory.
93	    """
94	    backend = "postgres"
95	    try:
96	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
97	        backend = (load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}).get("backend", "postgres")
98	    except Exception:
99	        backend = "postgres"
100	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=(backend == "memory"))
101	
102	
103	# ── Checkpointer factory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
104	
105	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
106	    """
107	    Return a configured checkpointer.
108	
109	    Args:
110	        use_memory: If True, return MemorySaver (Phase 0 testing, no Postgres needed).
111	                    If False, return PostgresSaver (Phase 1+ production).
112	
113	    Returns:
114	        MemorySaver | PostgresSaver
115	    """
116	    if use_memory:
117	        return MemorySaver()
118	
119	    try:
120	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
121	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
122	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
123	    except ImportError as e:
124	        raise ImportError(
125	            "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres and psycopg[pool] are required for PostgresSaver. "
126	            "Run: pip install 'langgraph-checkpoint-postgres' 'psycopg[binary,pool]'"
127	        ) from e
128	
129	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
130	    try:
131	        # A single raw Connection has no recovery: when the Postgres backend goes
132	        # away (node sleep/restart, idle timeout, network blip) the cached
133	        # connection is permanently broken and every subsequent checkpoint op
134	        # raises — silent loss of durability exactly during interrupt/resume across
135	        # the approval gates. Use a self-healing ConnectionPool instead: it runs a
136	        # liveness check on checkout and discards/replaces dead connections.
137	        # PostgresSaver natively accepts a ConnectionPool. (Hardening pass 1, #4.)
138	        pool = ConnectionPool(
139	            conn_str,
140	            min_size=1,
141	            max_size=8,
142	            kwargs={
143	                "autocommit": True,
144	                "prepare_threshold": 0,
145	                "row_factory": dict_row,
146	            },
147	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,
148	            open=True,
149	        )
150	        # PostgresSaver does not own the pool, so close it cleanly at process exit
151	        # (otherwise the pool's worker thread cannot be joined during interpreter
152	        # finalization, raising a noisy PythonFinalizationError). A single shared
153	        # atexit handler closes all pools — no per-call accumulation (#249).
154	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
155	        # Serialize schema migrations across processes with a session advisory
156	        # lock (pass-1 #33). The lock and the migration DDL MUST run on the SAME
157	        # connection — pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped, and a pool-backed
158	        # PostgresSaver.setup() would check out a *different* connection for the
159	        # DDL, leaving the lock ineffective (pass-3 #2). So run setup() through a
160	        # saver bound to the single locked connection, then return the pool-backed
161	        # saver for normal operation.
162	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. setup() is idempotent
163	        # but a redundant advisory lock + migration SELECT on every run start is
164	        # wasteful; the lock still protects the first-time cross-process race (#276).
165	        # _init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads
166	        # don't both run setup() (pass-5); the held lock also serializes the DDL,
167	        # which is fine — it runs at most once per conn_str per process.
168	        with _init_lock:
169	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
170	                with pool.connection() as conn:
171	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
172	                    try:
173	                        PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
174	                    finally:
175	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
176	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
177	        return PostgresSaver(pool)
178	    except Exception as e:
179	        raise RuntimeError(
180	            f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {conn_str}? "
181	            f"Error: {e}\n"
182	            "For Phase 0 testing without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
183	        ) from e
184

content.text: These are just configuration field names, not actual concurrent tests. Let me check the benchmark file:

content.content:
1	"""
2	bench_checkpointer.py — PostgresSaver throughput benchmark.
3	
4	Phase 0 deliverable #4: measure checkpoint write/read throughput on target hardware.
5	Uses the graph's public API (.invoke, .get_state) — NOT the internal checkpointer.put()
6	API, which is unstable across LangGraph minor versions.
7	
8	Results are logged to MLflow (local tracking, no SaaS).
9	
10	Run with:
11	    python -m pytest tests/benchmarks/bench_checkpointer.py -v -s
12	    -- or --
13	    python tests/benchmarks/bench_checkpointer.py
14	
15	Prerequisites (PostgresSaver path):
16	    - PostgreSQL running on localhost:5432/hcgrc
17	    - HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var (optional, defaults to localhost)
18	
19	If Postgres is not available, the PostgresSaver benchmark is skipped (pytest) or
20	reports "unavailable" (standalone runner); the MemorySaver baseline always runs.
21	MemorySaver results are not representative of production throughput.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import math
27	import time
28	import uuid
29	import warnings
30	from typing import Any
31	
32	import pytest
33	
34	from src.checkpointer import get_checkpointer
35	from src.graph import build_graph
36	from src.state import initial_state
37	
38	
39	def _percentile(sorted_vals: list[float], pct: float) -> float:
40	    """Nearest-rank percentile. Fixes the off-by-one that made p95 == max (#211)."""
41	    if not sorted_vals:
42	        return 0.0
43	    rank = max(1, math.ceil(pct / 100.0 * len(sorted_vals)))
44	    return sorted_vals[min(rank, len(sorted_vals)) - 1]
45	
46	
47	# ── Benchmark parameters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48	
49	N_RUNS = 20            # Number of graph runs (each = one write + one read cycle)
50	WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS = 150  # Each checkpoint write < 150ms on target hardware
51	READ_THRESHOLD_MS = 75    # Each state read < 75ms on target hardware
52	
53	
54	# ── Benchmark core ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55	
56	def run_benchmark(use_memory: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]:
57	    """
58	    Execute the checkpointer benchmark via the graph's public API.
59	
60	    Each iteration:
61	      1. Invokes the graph up to the Gate 1 interrupt (triggers a checkpoint write)
62	      2. Reads the state snapshot back (exercises the checkpoint read path)
63	
64	    Timing is wall-clock around each step.
65	    """
66	    # Measure checkpoint write/read, NOT LLM inference: the orchestrator node would
67	    # otherwise call the reasoning_client and fold model latency into the timing
68	    # (#234). Disable reasoning + tracing for the duration of the benchmark.
69	    import os
70	    os.environ["HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING"] = "1"
71	    os.environ["HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING"] = "1"
72	
73	    checkpointer = get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
74	    backend = "MemorySaver" if use_memory else "PostgresSaver"
75	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
76	
77	    write_times_ms: list[float] = []
78	    read_times_ms: list[float] = []
79	
80	    for _ in range(N_RUNS):
81	        run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
82	        state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
83	        config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": run_id}}
84	
85	        # Write: invoke graph until Gate 1 interrupt — checkpointer writes state
86	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
87	        graph.invoke(state, config=config)
88	        write_times_ms.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
89	
90	        # Read: get_state reads the most recent checkpoint
91	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
92	        snapshot = graph.get_state(config)
93	        read_times_ms.append((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
94	
95	        assert snapshot is not None, "Checkpoint read returned None"
96	        assert snapshot.values.get("run_id") == run_id, "run_id mismatch after checkpoint round-trip"
97	
98	    write_sorted = sorted(write_times_ms)
99	    read_sorted = sorted(read_times_ms)
100	
101	    return {
102	        "backend": backend,
103	        "n_runs": N_RUNS,
104	        "write_p50_ms": _percentile(write_sorted, 50),
105	        "write_p95_ms": _percentile(write_sorted, 95),
106	        "write_max_ms": max(write_times_ms),
107	        "read_p50_ms": _percentile(read_sorted, 50),
108	        "read_p95_ms": _percentile(read_sorted, 95),
109	        "read_max_ms": max(read_times_ms),
110	        "write_threshold_ms": WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS,
111	        "read_threshold_ms": READ_THRESHOLD_MS,
112	        "write_threshold_passed": all(t < WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS for t in write_times_ms),
113	        "read_threshold_passed": all(t < READ_THRESHOLD_MS for t in read_times_ms),
114	    }
115	
116	
117	# ── MLflow logging ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
118	
119	def log_to_mlflow(results: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
120	    """Log benchmark results to MLflow (local tracking server, no SaaS)."""
121	    try:
122	        import mlflow
123	        with mlflow.start_run(run_name=f"checkpointer_benchmark_{results['backend']}"):
124	            mlflow.log_params({
125	                "backend": results["backend"],
126	                "n_runs": results["n_runs"],
127	                "write_threshold_ms": results["write_threshold_ms"],
128	                "read_threshold_ms": results["read_threshold_ms"],
129	            })
130	            mlflow.log_metrics({
131	                "write_p50_ms": results["write_p50_ms"],
132	                "write_p95_ms": results["write_p95_ms"],
133	                "write_max_ms": results["write_max_ms"],
134	                "read_p50_ms": results["read_p50_ms"],
135	                "read_p95_ms": results["read_p95_ms"],
136	                "read_max_ms": results["read_max_ms"],
137	            })
138	    except Exception as e:
139	        warnings.warn(f"MLflow logging failed (non-critical): {e}")
140	
141	
142	# ── pytest integration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143	
144\[REDACTED]
145	class TestCheckpointerBenchmark:
146	    """Phase 0 checkpointer throughput benchmark (pytest entry point)."""
147	
148	    def test_memory_saver_benchmark(self):
149	        """MemorySaver baseline — always runs, no Postgres required."""
150	        results = run_benchmark(use_memory=True)
151	        print(f"\nMemorySaver: write p50={results['write_p50_ms']:.1f}ms  "
152	              f"read p50={results['read_p50_ms']:.1f}ms")
153	        assert results["write_p50_ms"] < 500, (
154	            f"MemorySaver write p50 {results['write_p50_ms']:.0f}ms unexpectedly slow — "
155	            "check for regressions in graph compilation"
156	        )
157	
158	    @pytest.mark.skipif(
159	        not __import__("os").environ.get("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL")
160	        and not __import__("shutil").which("psql"),
161	        reason="PostgreSQL not available — set HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL to run",
162	    )
163	    def test_postgres_saver_benchmark(self):
164	        """PostgresSaver benchmark — requires Postgres on localhost."""
165	        results = run_benchmark(use_memory=False)
166	        log_to_mlflow(results)
167	        print(f"\nPostgresSaver: write p50={results['write_p50_ms']:.1f}ms "
168	              f"(threshold: {WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS}ms)  "
169	              f"read p50={results['read_p50_ms']:.1f}ms "
170	              f"(threshold: {READ_THRESHOLD_MS}ms)")
171	        assert results["write_p95_ms"] < WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS, (
172	            f"Write p95 {results['write_p95_ms']:.1f}ms exceeds {WRITE_THRESHOLD_MS}ms"
173	        )
174	        assert results["read_p95_ms"] < READ_THRESHOLD_MS, (
175	            f"Read p95 {results['read_p95_ms']:.1f}ms exceeds {READ_THRESHOLD_MS}ms"
176	        )
177	
178	
179	# ── Standalone runner ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
180	
181	if __name__ == "__main__":
182	    print(f"Running MemorySaver benchmark ({N_RUNS} runs)...")
183	    mem = run_benchmark(use_memory=True)
184	    print(f"  Write: p50={mem['write_p50_ms']:.1f}ms  p95={mem['write_p95_ms']:.1f}ms  "
185	          f"max={mem['write_max_ms']:.1f}ms")
186	    print(f"  Read:  p50={mem['read_p50_ms']:.1f}ms  p95={mem['read_p95_ms']:.1f}ms  "
187	          f"max={mem['read_max_ms']:.1f}ms")
188

190	        print(f"\nRunning PostgresSaver benchmark ({N_RUNS} runs)...")
191	        pg = run_benchmark(use_memory=False)
192	        log_to_mlflow(pg)

196	        print(f"  Read:  p50={pg['read_p50_ms']:.1f}ms  p95={pg['read_p95_ms']:.1f}ms  "
197	              f"(threshold: {READ_THRESHOLD_MS}ms) — "

content.text: Now let me check the gates_hardening test to see if there are concurrent tests there:

content.content:
def test_gate_id_correlation_rejects_mismatched_decision():
    # pass-4 #3/#5: a resume decision targeted at a different gate must NOT finalize
    # the parked gate — it re-prompts. resume_run now carries gate_id end to end.
    cp = MemorySaver()
    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=cp)
    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "corr-001"}}
    graph.invoke(initial_state(run_id="corr-001"), config=cfg)  # parks at gate_1

    # Decision aimed at gate_2 while gate_1 is parked → ignored, gate_1 undecided.
    resume_run("corr-001", "approved", "x", cp, gate_id="gate_2")
    assert "gate_1" not in build_graph(checkpointer=cp).get_state(cfg).values.get("gate_status", {})

    # Correctly targeted decision finalizes.
    resume_run("corr-001", "approved", "ok", cp, gate_id="gate_1")
    assert build_graph(checkpointer=cp).get_state(cfg).values["gate_status"]["gate_1"]["decision"] == "approved"


# ── Gate 2 prerequisite failure writes a visible decision (#164 / #178) ────────


def test_gate2_prereq_failure_writes_gate_status():
--


def test_resume_run_approves_gate_1():
    cp = MemorySaver()
    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=cp)
    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "resume-001"}}
    graph.invoke(initial_state(run_id="resume-001"), config=cfg)  # parks at gate_1

    resume_run("resume-001", "approved", "prerequisites met", cp)

    decision = build_graph(checkpointer=cp).get_state(cfg).values["gate_status"]["gate_1"]["decision"]
    assert decision == "approved"


def test_resume_run_recovers_from_malformed_decision():
    # pass-3 #3: a malformed resume must NOT brick the gate — it re-prompts, and a
    # subsequent well-formed resume finalizes the gate (recovery actually works).
    cp = MemorySaver()
    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=cp)
    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "resume-002"}}
    graph.invoke(initial_state(run_id="resume-002"), config=cfg)  # parks at gate_1

    # Malformed decision: does not raise, does not record a terminal decision.
    resume_run("resume-002", "totally-bogus", "x", cp)
    parked = build_graph(checkpointer=cp).get_state(cfg).values
    assert "gate_1" not in parked.get("gate_status", {})

    # Well-formed decision now recovers and finalizes.
    resume_run("resume-002", "approved", "ok", cp)
    decided = build_graph(checkpointer=cp).get_state(cfg).values
    assert decided["gate_status"]["gate_1"]["decision"] == "approved"

91	def test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str(monkeypatch):

93	    # get_checkpointer() call. Counts real setup() invocations across two calls.

content.text: Now let me look at the implementation again to understand the _init_lock usage and whether concurrent access is actually tested:

content.content:
105	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
106	    """
107	    Return a configured checkpointer.
108	
109	    Args:
110	        use_memory: If True, return MemorySaver (Phase 0 testing, no Postgres needed).
111	                    If False, return PostgresSaver (Phase 1+ production).
112	
113	    Returns:
114	        MemorySaver | PostgresSaver
115	    """
116	    if use_memory:
117	        return MemorySaver()
118	
119	    try:
120	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
121	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
122	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
123	    except ImportError as e:
124	        raise ImportError(
125	            "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres and psycopg[pool] are required for PostgresSaver. "
126	            "Run: pip install 'langgraph-checkpoint-postgres' 'psycopg[binary,pool]'"
127	        ) from e
128	
129	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
130	    try:
131	        # A single raw Connection has no recovery: when the Postgres backend goes
132	        # away (node sleep/restart, idle timeout, network blip) the cached
133	        # connection is permanently broken and every subsequent checkpoint op
134	        # raises — silent loss of durability exactly during interrupt/resume across
135	        # the approval gates. Use a self-healing ConnectionPool instead: it runs a
136	        # liveness check on checkout and discards/replaces dead connections.
137	        # PostgresSaver natively accepts a ConnectionPool. (Hardening pass 1, #4.)
138	        pool = ConnectionPool(
139	            conn_str,
140	            min_size=1,
141	            max_size=8,
142	            kwargs={
143	                "autocommit": True,
144	                "prepare_threshold": 0,
145	                "row_factory": dict_row,
146	            },
147	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,
148	            open=True,
149	        )
150	        # PostgresSaver does not own the pool, so close it cleanly at process exit
151	        # (otherwise the pool's worker thread cannot be joined during interpreter
152	        # finalization, raising a noisy PythonFinalizationError). A single shared
153	        # atexit handler closes all pools — no per-call accumulation (#249).
154	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
155	        # Serialize schema migrations across processes with a session advisory
156	        # lock (pass-1 #33). The lock and the migration DDL MUST run on the SAME
157	        # connection — pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped, and a pool-backed
158	        # PostgresSaver.setup() would check out a *different* connection for the
159	        # DDL, leaving the lock ineffective (pass-3 #2). So run setup() through a
160	        # saver bound to the single locked connection, then return the pool-backed
161	        # saver for normal operation.
162	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. setup() is idempotent
163	        # but a redundant advisory lock + migration SELECT on every run start is
164	        # wasteful; the lock still protects the first-time cross-process race (#276).
165	        # _init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads
166	        # don't both run setup() (pass-5); the held lock also serializes the DDL,
167	        # which is fine — it runs at most once per conn_str per process.
168	        with _init_lock:
169	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
170	                with pool.connection() as conn:
171	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
172	                    try:
173	                        PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
174	                    finally:
175	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
176	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
177	        return PostgresSaver(pool)
178	    except Exception as e:
179	        raise RuntimeError(
180	            f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {conn_str}? "
181	            f"Error: {e}\n"
182	            "For Phase 0 testing without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
183	        ) from e

content.content:
1	"""
2	Checkpointer factory tests (hardening pass 1, #161).
3	
4	MemorySaver is always exercised. The PostgresSaver path (self-healing pool) is
5	exercised and round-tripped against real Postgres. In CI a Postgres service
6	container is provisioned (see .github/workflows/test.yml), so these run there too;
7	locally they skip only when no database is reachable (#260).
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import os
13	
14	import pytest
15	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
16	
17	from src.checkpointer import get_checkpointer, get_postgres_connection_string
18	from src.state import initial_state
19	
20	# GitHub Actions sets GITHUB_ACTIONS=true. In CI the durability/recovery invariant
21	# MUST be exercised, not silently skipped — a plain skipif on a DB-less runner left
22	# the cross-boundary recovery contract unverified exactly where regressions land
23	# (#260). Locally (no CI), missing Postgres still skips so contributors without a
24	# database can run the rest of the suite.
25	_IN_CI = os.environ.get("GITHUB_ACTIONS", "").lower() == "true"
26	
27	
28	def test_memory_checkpointer():
29	    cp = get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)
30	    assert isinstance(cp, MemorySaver)
31	
32	
33	def _postgres_reachable() -> bool:
34	    try:
35	        import psycopg
36	        with psycopg.connect(get_postgres_connection_string(), connect_timeout=2):
37	            return True
38	    except Exception:
39	        return False
40	
41	
42	def _require_postgres() -> None:
43	    """Skip locally when Postgres is absent, but FAIL in CI — the durability
44	    invariant must never silently skip on the runner where regressions slip in.
45	
46	    Evaluated at call time (in the test body), not at import time like a skipif
47	    decorator, so a service container that finishes coming up just after collection
48	    is still picked up (#260)."""
49	    if _postgres_reachable():
50	        return
51	    if _IN_CI:
52	        pytest.fail(
53	            "Postgres is unreachable in CI — the checkpointer durability/recovery "
54	            "tests must run here (provision the service container and set "
55	            "HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL). The cross-boundary recovery invariant must not "
56	            "silently skip in CI (#260)."
57	        )
58	    pytest.skip("Postgres not reachable (local run without a database)")
59	
60	
61	def test_postgres_checkpointer_round_trips():
62	    _require_postgres()
63	    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
64	
65	    cp = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
66	    assert isinstance(cp, PostgresSaver)
67	    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "test-cp-roundtrip", "checkpoint_ns": ""}}
68	    # list() must succeed against the pooled connection (no error == healthy pool).
69	    assert isinstance(list(cp.list(cfg)), list)
70	
71	
72	def test_checkpoint_recovers_across_a_fresh_checkpointer():
73	    # Durability across a process/connection boundary (#224): write a checkpoint
74	    # with one checkpointer/pool, then read it back through a SEPARATE checkpointer
75	    # (new pool) — simulating a process restart resuming a parked run.
76	    _require_postgres()
77	    from src.graph import build_graph
78	
79	    run_id = "test-cp-recovery"
80	    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": run_id}}
81	
82	    cp_write = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
83	    build_graph(checkpointer=cp_write).invoke(initial_state(run_id=run_id), config=cfg)
84	
85	    cp_read = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)  # fresh pool / different connections
86	    recovered = build_graph(checkpointer=cp_read).get_state(cfg)
87	    assert recovered.values.get("run_id") == run_id
88	    assert recovered.next  # still parked at the Gate 1 interrupt
89	
90	
91	def test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str(monkeypatch):
92	    # #276 (pass-5): setup() must run once per process per conn_str, not on every
93	    # get_checkpointer() call. Counts real setup() invocations across two calls.
94	    _require_postgres()
95	    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
96	
97	    import src.checkpointer as cp_mod
98	
99	    cp_mod._INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.clear()
100	    calls = {"n": 0}
101	    orig = PostgresSaver.setup
102	
103	    def counting(self):
104	        calls["n"] += 1
105	        return orig(self)
106	
107	    monkeypatch.setattr(PostgresSaver, "setup", counting)
108	    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
109	    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
110	    assert calls["n"] == 1  # second call hits the _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS guard
111	
112	
113	def test_get_checkpointer_raises_helpful_error_on_connection_failure(monkeypatch):
114	    # The error path (lines wrapping ConnectionPool/setup) must surface the
115	    # operator-facing RuntimeError with the "is Postgres running" guidance. Mocks
116	    # ConnectionPool so it needs no real database.
117	    import psycopg_pool
118	
119	    def boom(*a, **k):
120	        raise RuntimeError("connection refused")
121	
122	    monkeypatch.setattr(psycopg_pool, "ConnectionPool", boom)
123	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="PostgresSaver connection failed"):
124	        get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
125

content.text: Now let me check what the _register_pool_cleanup function does with the lock:

content.content:
<tool_use_error>InputValidationError: Read failed due to the following issue:
The parameter `offset` type is expected as `number` but provided as `array`</tool_use_error>

1	"""
2	PostgresSaver configuration for HC-GRC.
3	
4	LangGraph uses the checkpointer to persist graph state between turns,
5	enabling interrupt/resume across the operator approval gates.
6	
7	Per ADR-0015 (#79): run_id propagates to PostgresSaver as thread_id.
8	Cross-store query procedure in RUNBOOK.md §5.
9	
10	LOCAL-FIRST CONSTRAINT: PostgreSQL instance runs on-device.
11	No state leaves the machine. SaaS checkpointers are disqualified.
12	
13	Connection config:
14	  Database: hcgrc
15	  Host: localhost (default)
16	  Port: 5432 (default)
17	
18	For Phase 0 testing: use MemorySaver (no Postgres required).
19	For Phase 1+: use PostgresSaver with the connection string below.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import atexit
25	import os
26	import threading
27	
28	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
29	
30	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes
31	# checkpoint schema migrations across concurrent initializers (get_checkpointer).
32	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4843_4752  # "HCGR"
33	
34	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping below. The PG advisory lock
35	# serializes the DDL across PROCESSES, but the in-memory check-and-set guards
36	# (_atexit_registered, _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS) are not atomic on their own — two
37	# threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and
38	# double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup(). This lock makes those
39	# check-and-set sequences atomic within the process (pass-5).
40	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
41	
42	# Pools opened by get_checkpointer, closed once at process exit. A single atexit
43	# handler closes them all — registering one per call would accumulate handlers
44	# across repeated get_checkpointer() calls (#249).
45	_OPEN_POOLS: list = []
46	_atexit_registered = False
47	
48	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL + migration SELECT)
49	# has already run in this process. setup() is idempotent but not free — re-running
50	# it on every get_checkpointer() call takes a global advisory lock and replays the
51	# migration version query at each run start. Bootstrap once per process per schema;
52	# the advisory lock still guards the first-time cross-process migration race (#276).
53	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
54	
55	
56	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool) -> None:
57	    global _atexit_registered
58	    with _init_lock:
59	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
60	        if not _atexit_registered:
61	            atexit.register(lambda: [p.close() for p in _OPEN_POOLS])
62	            _atexit_registered = True
63

content.content: 46a67d3 fix(hardening-p5): batch 16 — clear all 29 pass-5 findings (reviewed, 0 problems)

commit 46a67d36c2b033fc519173ceb432f8f3db3ff714
Author: Thomas Jones <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Mon Jun 15 10:16:39 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p5): batch 16 — clear all 29 pass-5 findings (reviewed, 0 problems)
    
    Pass-5 re-audit (wf_049f05ea-b77, 12 auditors + adversarial verify) found 29
    confirmed (0C/6H/13M/10L); 8 rejected. Each fix adversarially reviewed
    (wf_6fb1dcd8-855, 14 reviewers) — 0 problems flagged. Several findings were
    flaws in batch-15 fixes (the loop working as intended).
    
    reasoning_client:
    - _submit_bounded decrements _inflight if pool.submit() raises (no slot leak)
    - complete_json records + propagates inner complete() errors (rate-limit/permanent)
      instead of re-trying them as JSON-parse failures
    - temperature retry bumps DOWN when the upward bump would exceed 1.0 — no
      saturation at high input temps (retries stay distinct + in range)
    - capture stop_reason on successful T3 runs -> llm.finish_reason; agent_id on the
      complete_json parent span
    
    langgraph:
    - checkpointer _init_lock makes the in-process atexit + setup-once check-and-sets
      thread-safe (advisory lock still serializes cross-process)
    - build_graph return type -> CompiledStateGraph (from langgraph.graph.state)
    - eda_artifacts reducer O(n+m) via set membership
    
    security / ADR-0002 data sovereignty:
    - consolidated the loopback guard into config.require_loopback / require_local_uri
      (shared by Phoenix, Qdrant, MLflow) + fail-fast validation at config load
    - phoenix_endpoint(), Qdrant get_qdrant_client(), MLflow configure_mlflow() now
      reject non-loopback / remote hosts (Qdrant + MLflow were real off-box holes)
    - docker-compose binds Qdrant to 127.0.0.1
    
    config / CI:
    - requirements-ci version-bound parity (psycopg>=3.2, pytest<9, pytest-cov<6)
    
    tests: +44 (timeout-pool leak, complete_json error/temperature, T3 rate-event &
    permission_mode, _run_sync branches, SIGTERM handler, provider-collision warning,
    every loopback guard, checkpointer setup-once + connection-failure, state reducers).

diff --git a/tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py b/tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py
index 211200f..cb7a15c 100644
--- a/tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py
+++ b/tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py
@@ -86,3 +86,39 @@ def test_checkpoint_recovers_across_a_fresh_checkpointer():
     recovered = build_graph(checkpointer=cp_read).get_state(cfg)
     assert recovered.values.get("run_id") == run_id
     assert recovered.next  # still parked at the Gate 1 interrupt
+
+
+def test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str(monkeypatch):
+    # #276 (pass-5): setup() must run once per process per conn_str, not on every
+    # get_checkpointer() call. Counts real setup() invocations across two calls.
+    _require_postgres()
+    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
+
+    import src.checkpointer as cp_mod
+
+    cp_mod._INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.clear()
+    calls = {"n": 0}
+    orig = PostgresSaver.setup
+
+    def counting(self):
+        calls["n"] += 1
+        return orig(self)
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(PostgresSaver, "setup", counting)
+    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
+    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
+    assert calls["n"] == 1  # second call hits the _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS guard
+
+
+def test_get_checkpointer_raises_helpful_error_on_connection_failure(monkeypatch):
+    # The error path (lines wrapping ConnectionPool/setup) must surface the
+    # operator-facing RuntimeError with the "is Postgres running" guidance. Mocks
+    # ConnectionPool so it needs no real database.
+    import psycopg_pool
+
+    def boom(*a, **k):
+        raise RuntimeError("connection refused")
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(psycopg_pool, "ConnectionPool", boom)
+    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="PostgresSaver connection failed"):
+        get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)

content.content: * hardening/pass-6

content.text:
Now let me synthesize my findings. I have all the information I need:

**Analysis Summary:**

1. **Code examined:**
   - `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/src/checkpointer.py` (lines 34-40, 56-62, 168-177) - the `_init_lock` implementation
   - `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py` (lines 91-110) - the test in question

2. **What the code ACTUALLY does:**
   - Line 40: `_init_lock = threading.Lock()` is defined
   - Lines 168-177: The lock is used to protect a critical section that checks and updates shared state (`_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS`)
   - Lines 56-62: The lock is ALSO used in `_register_pool_cleanup()` to protect the atexit registration
   - The comments explicitly state: "two threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup()"

3. **What the test ACTUALLY tests:**
   - Lines 108-110: Sequential calls to `get_checkpointer()` twice
   - Asserts that `setup()` is called exactly once
   - No concurrent/threaded access tested

4. **The Critical Finding:**
   - The code explicitly documents (lines 34-39, 165-167) that concurrent threads are a concern
   - The implementation uses `threading.Lock()` to protect against concurrent access
   - BUT the test `test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str` ONLY tests sequential calls
   - There is NO test spawning multiple threads to verify the lock actually works under concurrent access
   - This is a real gap: the lock could be buggy or missing in critical places, and the test wouldn't catch it

5. **Is this already mitigated elsewhere?**
   - I checked for other concurrent tests in the test suite
   - Only `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/tests/test_observability.py` imports threading, but only to mock `threading.current_thread()` (not an actual concurrent test)
   - No actual concurrent/threading test exists for `get_checkpointer()` initialization

6. **Is the severity correct?**
   - The candidate calls this "medium" severity
   - This is appropriate because: concurrent initialization of the checkpointer is a **real scenario** (multiple requests/threads could arrive simultaneously), the lock is **critical protection**, but the **absence of a test doesn't prove a bug exists** (the lock may work fine in practice)

7. **Confirmed verdict:**
   - This is a **REAL finding**: The test coverage gap is genuine and unmitigated
   - The critical path (concurrent threads calling `get_checkpointer()`) is explicitly protected by code but NOT tested
   - This violates the best practice that synchronization primitives should have explicit concurrent test coverage
   - The fix is straightforward and should use `threading.Thread` + barrier/event to ensure simultaneous invocation

input.title: Missing concurrent access test for checkpointer _init_lock
input.verification:
Verified the actual code:

src/checkpointer.py:40: `_init_lock = threading.Lock()` is defined and explicitly documented as protecting concurrent access (lines 34-39: "two threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup()").

src/checkpointer.py:168-177: The lock is used in `get_checkpointer()` to protect a critical section that checks and updates `_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS`. Lines 165-167 document: "_init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup()".

tests/test_phase0/test_postgres_checkpointer.py:91-110: The test `test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str` calls `get_checkpointer()` twice SEQUENTIALLY (lines 108-109) and verifies setup() runs once. No concurrent/threaded access is tested.

Searched entire test suite: No other concurrent tests exist for checkpointer initialization (test_observability.py only mocks threading.current_thread(), it doesn't spawn actual threads).

Conclusion: The test gap is REAL and UNMITIGATED. The code explicitly documents and protects against concurrent thread access, but the test suite only covers sequential access. This violates the best practice that synchronization primitives must have explicit concurrent scenario coverage.
input.recommended_action:
Add test_get_checkpointer_concurrent_threads (or similar) that:
1. Clears _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS
2. Monkeypatches PostgresSaver.setup to count invocations
3. Spawns N threads (e.g., 5-10) that call get_checkpointer() simultaneously
4. Uses threading.Barrier to ensure near-simultaneous invocation
5. Asserts setup() was called exactly once despite concurrent access
6. Verifies all threads received a valid PostgresSaver instance

This directly tests the _init_lock's protection of the check-and-set race condition under realistic concurrent load.
